new_value: Replaced hardcoded 150ms sleep with polling loop that verifies target app is frontmost before pasting (50ms polls, 500ms timeout). Logs a warning if activation fails.

{"description":"Implement tiered paste system to replace single-method CGEvent Cmd+V approach. Validated by Gemini + OpenAI.

ARCHITECTURE (ordered cascade):
- Tier 1: AX direct insertion via kAXSelectedTextAttribute on captured AXFocusedUIElement (zero disruption, no clipboard, no focus change)
- Tier 2: Activate app + CGEvent Cmd+V via .cgAnnotatedSessionEventTap (current approach, refined with AXObserver-based paste confirmation)
- Tier 2b: AppleScript Edit > Paste (sandboxed app fallback, requires frontmost)  
- Tier 3: Clipboard + user notification (graceful failure, text never lost)

KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Capture AXFocusedUIElement at PTT start (alongside existing NSRunningApplication)
- All AX calls on MainActor (AXUIElement is Sendable but operations are not thread-safe)
- Set AXTimeout(1.0s) before AX writes to avoid 30s hang on misbehaving apps
- Verify kAXRoleAttribute == textField/textArea before Tier 1 attempt
- Log (bundle_id, successful_tier, duration_ms) for compatibility analytics
- Switch CGEvent tap from .cghidEventTap to .cgAnnotatedSessionEventTap

FILES TO MODIFY:
1. TranscriptionPipeline.swift — add targetElement capture + tiered paste cascade
2. PasteService.swift — add insertViaAccessibility(), pasteViaAppleScript(), switch event tap, add AXObserver paste confirmation
3. Constants.swift — paste confirmation polling constants

SOURCES: Hex (kitlangton/Hex), speak2 (zachswift615/speak2), Gemini, OpenAI","status":"in_progress","title":"Implement tiered paste system (AX direct → CGEvent → AppleScript → clipboard)"}

Indented fences or language-tagged fences not at column 0 could cause false-positive broken link reports. One false positive already hit and worked around. Fix is a 1-line regex improvement — do it next time brain-check.sh is touched for any reason. Not worth a standalone change.","priority":4}

{"id":"ew-7tt","title":"[Q10] brain-check.sh: code-fence stripping regex is naive","description":"Finding Q10: sed '/^```/,/^```/d' assumes fences start at column 0 with exactly 3 backticks. Indented fences or fences with language tags that don't start at column 0 would be missed, causing false-positive broken link detections.

DEFERRED: Current brain files use standard fences. No false positives observed except the one already fixed.
UPSIDE: More robust code-fence detection, fewer false positives as brain files grow.
CHURN RISK: Low — regex improvement, but more complex regex is harder to maintain.
REVISIT TRIGGER: If brain-check.sh reports false-positive broken links from code examples in brain files.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:42:15Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:42:15Z"}

(4) Guard against identical intents to prevent flicker. (5) Migrate both pipelines in one commit, delete legacy API after.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T17:20:26Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:21:55Z"}

old_value: {"id":"ew-d17","title":"Pipeline race: rapid PTT re-press during polish causes permanent 0.00s recording","description":"When user presses PTT again while pipeline is still processing a previous recording (polish/paste phase), the pipeline enters a bad state where all subsequent recordings are 0.00s and immediately discarded. Requires app restart to recover. Root cause: pipeline doesn't fully reset before accepting new recording when mid-polish. Seen in logs: PTT press at 18:59:44 while previous recording's polish was in-flight, then all subsequent attempts are 0.00s. Phase 1 bug cleanup item.","notes":"App development bug — phase 1 cleanup alongside ew-pe9, ew-9bd. NOT part of brain surgery epic (ew-cbo).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T19:02:16Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T19:03:05Z"}

new_value: Fixed PTT race: (1) clear stopRequested at startRecording() entry to wipe stale flags from previous cycles, (2) tighten requestStop() to only set flag during .idle/.loadingModel — ignore during .polishing/.transcribing/.complete/.error. Root cause: key-up during polish set stopRequested, which persisted and aborted every subsequent recording.

old_value: {"id":"ew-9bd","title":"LLMPolishStep ignores language — blocks WhisperKit multilingual","description":"ChatterBox reality check found: LLMPolishStep never reads context.language. TranscriptPolisher.polish() has no language parameter. PolishInstructions has no language field. For English-only Parakeet this is invisible, but WhisperKit multilingual will send Japanese/French/German text to an English-assuming polish prompt, corrupting output. Fix: read context.language, prepend language context to prompt when non-en. File: LLMPolishStep.swift (~113 lines). This is the ONE adaptation needed at the merge point.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:40:20Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:40:20Z"}

new_value: LLMPolishStep now reads context.language via enrichedInstructions() — single enrichment point for all context-aware prompt modifications. Non-English transcripts get LANGUAGE preamble prepended to any prompt (default or custom). No protocol/connector changes. extensible for future guards (ew-zr4 short-transcript).

old_value: {"id":"ew-zr4","title":"LLM polish hallucinates on ultra-short transcripts (e.g. 'Yeah')","description":"When ASR captures only a filler word like 'Yeah' (accidental short recording), the LLM polish step generates a full conversational response instead of passing through or discarding. Screenshot shows 'Yeah.' becoming a multi-paragraph speech about public speaking. Root cause: polish prompt gives the LLM too much creative latitude on minimal input. Fix options: (1) short-circuit polish for transcripts under N words/chars, (2) add explicit instruction in polish prompt to preserve ultra-short inputs verbatim, (3) both. This was not an issue before — may be related to a model change (appears to be Gemini from the style). Post-highway cleanup item.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T21:13:40Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:13:40Z"}

new_value: Two-layer fix: (1) hard cutoff ≤3 words skips LLM entirely — pass through verbatim, (2) prompt reinforcement for 4-10 words tells LLM to return short inputs as-is. Both in LLMPolishStep. Thresholds tunable via minWordsForPolish constant.

old_value: {"id":"ew-hk6","title":"Refactor: eliminate stopRequested flag — add .startingUp state to pipeline state machines","description":"The stopRequested boolean is a side-channel that operates outside the pipeline state machine. It was the root cause of ew-d17 (PTT race → permanent 0.00s recordings). Current fix (clear on entry + tighten requestStop) is correct but the pattern is fragile — every new await in startRecording() needs a manual checkpoint, and the flag is invisible to the state machine. Refactor: add .startingUp state to WhisperKitPipelineState and PipelineState. requestStop() during .startingUp transitions directly to .idle — no boolean flag needed. Eliminates the entire class of stale-flag bugs. Should be done before Phase 2/3 (VAD, streaming) adds more async checkpoints to startRecording().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"saurabhav@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T21:19:26Z","created_by":"saurabhav88","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:19:26Z"}
